title: "streaming output" test: constant flakes on ubuntu
body:
The [Podman logs [It] streaming output test](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/d6965da26d194c7edfef65985d4a2c7b564a3fca/test/e2e/logs_test.go#L275) is broken on Ubuntu, or maybe it's reporting a real problem. It is flaking frequently in CI. Only in Ubuntu, AFAICT. 

Symptom: the `log -f` terminates successfully, then the next `podman run` (with same name) fails:
```
       Running: podman [options] run --rm --name logs-f-rm -dt docker.io/library/alpine:latest true
         Error: error creating container storage: the container name "logs-f-rm" is already in use by "6daa668028d4270d9d4bcfc66c415ce6a2a56b70e0b5753f9064dc46f7047da0". You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name.: that name is already in use
```
Full history from cirrus-flake-xref:

* ubuntu-19 : test ubuntu-19
  * PR #6615
    * [06-16 06:04](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/6499551370543104/html/integration_test.log.html#t--streaming-output--1)
    * [06-16 06:04](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/6499551370543104/html/integration_test.log.html#t--streaming-output--2)

Best guess is that `log -f` does not actually wait for container cleanup. But why would this be only on Ubuntu?

comments.body: Are we using the same version of conmon?
There's a newer Conmon on Fedora. Looking to remedy this in Ubuntu with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6560
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon @vrothberg Should we close this, referenced PRs have been merged?
I agree. Please reopen if I am mistaken.
